Title: Spatiotemporal deposition of cell wall polysaccharides in oat endosperm during grain development

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like F (CSLF) family; glycosyltransferase GT2-like cellulose synthase superfamily
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: MLG synthase module of cereal endosperm cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes mixed-linkage (1,3;1,4)-β-D-glucan polymer in the cell wall from UDP-glucose-derived glucan units
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as major MLG synthase of cereals; key control point implied but not directly established here
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Soluble dietary fiber contributing to grain nutritional value and endosperm cell wall architecture
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Oat grain endosperm cell walls, especially subaleurone/aleurone interface at later stages; also maternal tissues earlier
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher CSLF6 transcript abundance trend in CDC Morrison is associated with greater MLG accumulation in mature grain than in AC Morgan
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLF6 homeologs were analyzed by RNA-seq during oat grain development in CDC Morrison and AC Morgan. 2. CSLF6 is described as the major MLG synthase of cereals. 3. CSLF6 expression trended higher in CDC Morrison from 15 to 25 DPE, coinciding with the cultivar having higher mature grain MLG content. 4. The paper suggests MLG biosynthetic gene transcript abundance, including CSLF6, may contribute to greater MLG accumulation in CDC Morrison.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Developmental RNA-seq expression profiling; comparison of transcript abundance between cultivars with contrasting MLG content; metabolite/polysaccharide immunolabeling and biochemical MLG quantification
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane (inferred for MLG synthase); possible vesicle trafficking to plasma membrane discussed
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during grain development, with trend of higher expression at 15–25 DPE in CDC Morrison
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Whole developing grains from 5 to 40 DPE; expression present before and during onset of endosperm MLG deposition
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing grain stages 5–40 DPE under controlled glasshouse conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for breeding or engineering higher oat MLG/dietary fiber; likely most useful in combination with understanding deposition pattern and cell wall architecture
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher MLG cultivar CDC Morrison showed lower starch content, smaller endosperm cells, and generally lower yield/kernel weight than AC Morgan
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study did not detect major statistically significant differences in AsCslF6 homeolog expression between cultivars, indicating that mechanisms beyond transcript level may explain contrasting deposition patterns; discussion proposes possible differences in CSLF6 delivery/turnover or primary sequence

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like H (CSLH) family; glycosyltransferase GT2-like cellulose synthase superfamily
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative synthesis of mixed-linkage (1,3;1,4)-β-D-glucan polymer in grasses
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Soluble dietary fiber and structural wall polysaccharide of oat endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher CSLH1 homeolog expression in CDC Morrison is associated with greater MLG accumulation in mature grain
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLH1 homeolog expression increased sharply during grain development. 2. This increase was significantly greater in CDC Morrison than AC Morgan. 3. The data suggest higher transcript abundance of MLG biosynthetic genes, including CSLH1, may contribute to greater MLG accumulation in CDC Morrison.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Developmental RNA-seq expression profiling and cultivar comparison against MLG deposition/biochemical content
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Sharp increase during grain development; significantly higher in CDC Morrison than AC Morgan at relevant stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Whole developing grains, especially around 15–25 DPE and later developmental stages
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to raise oat MLG content, likely in combination with CSLF genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included among multiple CSLH genes with higher transcript levels in the higher-MLG cultivar; direct catalytic proof was not generated in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like F, H and J families; glycosyltransferase GT2-like cellulose synthase superfamily
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Genes previously implicated in synthesis of MLG cell wall polymer
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary fiber and cell wall structural component
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing grain endosperm cell walls; maternal tissues earlier
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher transcript levels of multiple CSLF and CSLH genes in CDC Morrison likely contribute to increased mature grain MLG abundance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RNA-seq surveyed CSLF, CSLH, CSLJ and GUB genes during grain development. 2. Several CSLF and CSLH genes showed higher expression and/or altered timing in CDC Morrison than in AC Morgan. 3. Most expression peaked before visible endosperm MLG deposition, implying activity in maternal tissues as well as endosperm-associated functions. 4. These transcript differences were proposed to contribute to cultivar differences in MLG amount and deposition pattern.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq profiling across 5–40 DPE plus comparison with microscopy-based MLG deposition and biochemical MLG measurements
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during grain development, often earlier than visible endosperm MLG deposition
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Whole developing grain time course 5–40 DPE
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Gene family-level targets for improving oat soluble fiber content and wall architecture
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High MLG associated with lower starch and lower yield traits in the compared cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This grouped entry captures the family-level conclusion emphasized in the abstract and conclusions; individual non-CSLF6/CSLH1 members were not functionally dissected in results text provided

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Cell wall polysaccharide turnover enzyme; common metabolic regulator via degradation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Soluble dietary fiber and structural cell wall polysaccharide
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing and mature grain endosperm cell walls
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GUB2 transcript levels trended higher in CDC Morrison despite higher MLG accumulation, suggesting MLG turnover may occur alongside or contribute to MLG deposition
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GUB2 genes encoding MLG-specific endohydrolases were profiled during oat grain development. 2. Their expression trended higher in CDC Morrison over the same period as higher CSLF6 expression. 3. This was paradoxical because CDC Morrison also accumulates more MLG. 4. The authors propose that MLG accumulation can occur under higher turnover conditions, or that GUB enzymes may play a biosynthesis-associated role analogous to Korrigan1 in cellulose formation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of MLG turnover, with a possible unexpected positive role in MLG deposition/homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Polysaccharide degradation/turnover; possible remodeling during biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: MLG degradation and possibly MLG biosynthesis-associated remodeling/homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Not established; possible homeostatic turnover implied
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during grain development; GUB2 trends higher in CDC Morrison than AC Morgan over period of elevated CSLF6 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq profiling across development and comparison with MLG deposition/content differences between cultivars
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning MLG turnover and final grain fiber architecture, but requires caution because higher expression coexists with higher MLG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating turnover enzymes may unpredictably alter wall structure, deposition pattern, or solubility
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a transcriptional regulator, it is a core metabolism-related gene discussed experimentally in relation to MLG accumulation; the paper explicitly raises a possible Korrigan-like supportive role in biosynthesis
